---
title: "OSC Brand â€” revealjs Demo"
subtitle: "brand.yml SCSS variables in action"
author: "LMU Open Science Center"
format: osc-brand-revealjs
---

## Palette Colors

The brand palette defines 12 colors in `brand.yml`. These are available as SCSS variables with a `$brand-` prefix.

| SCSS variable | Hex value |
|---|---|
| `$brand-lmu-green` | `#00883A` |
| `$brand-lmu-black` | `#232323` |
| `$brand-lmu-white` | `#FFF` |
| `$brand-lmu-darkgrey` / `$brand-lmu-darkgray` | `#626468` |
| `$brand-lmu-middlegrey` / `$brand-lmu-middlegray` | `#C0C1C3` |
| `$brand-lmu-lightgrey` / `$brand-lmu-lightgray` | `#E6E6E7` |
| `$brand-lmu-lichtgrau` | `#F5F5F5` |
| `$brand-lmu-blue` | `#0F1987` |
| `$brand-lmu-cyan` | `#009FE3` |
| `$brand-lmu-violet` | `#8C4091` |
| `$brand-lmu-red` | `#D71919` |
| `$brand-lmu-orange` | `#F18700` |

---

## Semantic Colors

Brand roles `primary`, `secondary`, `tertiary`, `success`, `info`, `warning`, and `danger` are mapped from palette entries. These feed into Bootstrap classes and Quarto components.

<span class="badge text-bg-primary">primary</span>
<span class="badge text-bg-secondary">secondary</span>
<span class="badge text-bg-tertiary">tertiary</span>
<span class="badge text-bg-success">success</span>
<span class="badge text-bg-info">info</span>
<span class="badge text-bg-warning">warning</span>
<span class="badge text-bg-danger">danger</span>

Buttons also pick up these roles:

<button type="button" class="btn btn-primary btn-sm">Primary</button>
<button type="button" class="btn btn-secondary btn-sm">Secondary</button>
<button type="button" class="btn btn-success btn-sm">Success</button>
<button type="button" class="btn btn-info btn-sm">Info</button>
<button type="button" class="btn btn-warning btn-sm">Warning</button>
<button type="button" class="btn btn-danger btn-sm">Danger</button>

These correspond to `$brand-primary`, `$brand-secondary`, etc.

---

## Typography

The brand uses **Roboto** (headings and body) and **Fira Code** (monospace).

### Heading level 1

#### Heading level 2

##### Heading level 3

###### Heading level 4

Body text uses the brand's base font family at the configured size and line height. The body color is set from `$brand-lmu-black` via `$body-color`.

Monospace text like `Fira Code` is used for inline code. A monospace block font stack is also configured.

The typography section of `brand.yml` controls `$font-family-sans-serif`, `$font-family-monospace`, and heading-specific variables like `$presentation-heading-font-weight`, `$presentation-heading-text-transform`, and heading sizes.

---

## Code Blocks

Code blocks are styled by variables like `$code-block-bg`, `$code-block-border-color`, and `$code-block-font-size`.

```r
library(tidyverse)

starwars |>
  filter(homeworld == "Tatooine") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = height, y = mass)) +
  geom_point()
```

Inline code â€” like `dplyr::filter()` â€” uses `$code-color` and `$code-bg` for styling.

---

## Callouts

Quarto callouts use the brand's semantic colors via `$callout-color-note`, `$callout-color-tip`, `$callout-color-caution`, `$callout-color-warning`, and `$callout-color-important`.

::: {.callout-note}
A note callout using the brand's info color (`$brand-cyan`).
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
A tip callout using the brand's success color (`$brand-green`).
:::

::: {.callout-caution}
A caution callout using the brand's warning color (`$brand-orange`).
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
A warning callout.
:::

::: {.callout-important}
An important callout using the brand's danger color (`$brand-red`).
:::

---

## Links & Selection

Here is a [link to example.com](https://example.com) styled with `$link-color`.

Select some of this text to see the `$selection-bg` and `$selection-color` styling applied to highlighted content.

Also notice that muted text, like this sentence, uses `$text-muted`.

---

## Brand Logo

The brand logo can be included via the `{{</* brand logo */>}}` shortcode.

{{< brand logo small >}} {{< brand logo medium >}}

The medium logo switches between light and dark variants depending on the slide background mode.

---

## Variable Reference

These SCSS variables are set in `revealjs-custom.scss` and control presentation appearance:

| Variable | Controls |
|---|---|
| `$body-color` | Default text color |
| `$link-color` | Link color |
| `$text-muted` | Muted/secondary text |
| `$link-color-hover` | Link hover state |
| `$selection-bg` | Text selection highlight |
| `$selection-color` | Text selection text |
| `$light-bg-text-color` | Text on light slides |
| `$light-bg-link-color` | Links on light slides |
| `$light-bg-code-color` | Code on light slides |
| `$dark-bg-text-color` | Text on dark slides |
| `$dark-bg-link-color` | Links on dark slides |
| `$dark-bg-code-color` | Code on dark slides |
| `$presentation-font-size-root` | Base font size |
| `$presentation-h1/2/3/4-font-size` | Heading sizes |
| `$presentation-heading-font-weight` | Heading weight |
| `$presentation-heading-text-transform` | Heading case |
| `$code-block-bg` | Code block background |
| `$code-block-border-color` | Code block border |
| `$code-block-font-size` | Code block font size |
| `$code-color` / `$code-bg` | Inline code styling |
| `$callout-color-*` | Callout accent colors |
